input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: drill down from Insights tallies to the sessions behind them" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

Direction #2 of the batch: make the Fleet Insights tallies **explorable**. Each row — top tool, most-common error, recommendation, recurring issue — is now clickable and expands inline to show **which sessions contributed it**. Clicking a session opens it (reusing the app's own sidebar selection).

The aggregators already visited every session; they just discarded provenance. This recovers it on demand.

## How

- **`FleetInsights.sessionsFor(category, key, sessions)`** (pure): returns `SessionRef(id, title)` for matching sessions, reusing the *exact* keying as `aggregate` (raw tool name / `errorKey` / `clean()`) so a drilled value matches the report byte-for-byte. `titleOf` prefers the analysis short title, else the first user prompt, else the id.
- Records `SessionRef`, `DrilldownResult(category, key, totalMatches, sessions)`. **`InsightsService.drilldown`** caps to 50 but reports the true `totalMatches`.
- **`GET /api/insights/sessions?flavor=&category=&key=`**.
- **`insights.js`**: bar rows carry `data-drill-category`/`data-drill-key`; clicking `.drill-bar` fetches and expands the session list inline (toggle, load-once, graceful inline error on failure); clicking a `.drill-session` opens it. `openSession` matches `.conv-item` by dataset (no built selector), so any id is safe.

## Tests

- `FleetInsightsTest` — per-category matchers + title precedence (short title > user prompt > id) + unknown-category/no-match empties
- `InsightsControllerTest` — endpoint round-trip (category/totalMatches/id/title)
- `insights.test.js` — row tagging, expand→render→open-session, inline failure
- `e2e/insights.spec.js` — drilling a real tool row reveals a contributing session

Verification: `./gradlew build` (Spotless + JUnit) ✅ · 81 frontend ✅ · 27 e2e ✅

## Review

A code-review pass found **no MUST-FIX**; it confirmed no injection/traversal (category/key are only string comparisons, never paths), full XSS-escaping, and correct cap math. Applied its SHOULD-FIX (robust `openSession` that can't throw on an odd id) and both nitpicks (summary guards matching `aggregate`, non-aliasing cap).

Note: Mine's workflows/failure-fixes could get the same drill-down later; this PR covers the Insights view.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
**#2 done** — Insights drill-down merged ([PR #26](https://github.com/scottdensmore/antigravity-brain-visualizer/pull/26), `bc5235f`). Click any tally row to see the sessions behind it and open them.

---

Now **#3: export the eval run history as CSV**, so the A/B trend from #22 can go into a spreadsheet. The history is already at `GET /api/eval/runs`; I'll add a CSV rendering + a download button. Let me check the store's list shape (already have `EvalRunSnapshot`) and mirror the mine-export download pattern. First, branch and add a pure CSV formatter.

input.command:
git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
feat: export eval run history as CSV

Add a "⬇ CSV" button to the Run history section (shown once at least one run is
saved) that downloads the saved eval runs as a spreadsheet-friendly file, so the
quality trend from the run history can be charted or analyzed outside the app.

- eval-export.js: pure historyCsv(history) — a header row plus one row per run
  (savedAt, flavor, modelLabel, counts, avgScore, judged + judge averages), with
  RFC-4180 quoting/escaping for fields containing commas or quotes.
- mine-export.js downloadText gains an optional mime arg (defaults to markdown),
  reused here with text/csv.
- eval.js wires the button to download eval-history-<flavor>.csv.

Frontend-only; builds from the already-fetched history (no new endpoint / no
re-eval). Tests: eval-export.test.js (header + rows, comma/quote escaping,
empty), eval.test.js (button gating + click downloads a .csv), e2e. All green
(build, 86 frontend, 27 e2e).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git push -u origin feat/eval-history-csv 2>&1 | tail -3
input.description: Commit and push CSV export branch
